afterward.lock.subtitle: Your continuation. Afterward.
afterward.onboard.subtitle: Let's set up your continuation.
afterward.onboard.step1.title: Step 1 / 4 · What is this?
afterward.onboard.step1.body: You'll set a master password, name 5 trustees, and we'll split it into 5 cryptographic shares (any 3 can reconstruct the full password). Your content stays encrypted until: you've been silent for 150 days and 3 trustees confirm you're gone.
afterward.onboard.step1.warning: ⚠ Your master password is never stored anywhere on this device. If you forget it and can't recover it from trustees' shares, your content is permanently lost. This is by design.
afterward.onboard.step2.title: Step 2 / 4 · Master password
afterward.onboard.step2.body: The only password you need to remember. Keep it simple if that's what you want — you choose the risk. If you're distributing shares to real trustees, make it longer.
afterward.onboard.pwd_min_len: Password must be at least 6 characters.
afterward.onboard.step3.title: Step 3 / 4 · 5 Trustees
afterward.onboard.step3.body: 5 people you trust. Any 3 can jointly confirm your passing and reconstruct your password. No single trustee can decrypt alone.
afterward.onboard.step4.title: Step 4 / 4 · Distribute shares
afterward.onboard.step4.body: Important: Copy each share and deliver it to the corresponding trustee (USB / paper QR / encrypted email). This screen will clear when you continue — you won't see these shares again.
afterward.onboard.step4.confirm: I have delivered all shares to the corresponding trustees. No copy remains on this device.
afterward.onboard.setup_done: Setup complete. Use your master password to unlock.
afterward.home.title: Status
afterward.heartbeat.title: Daily challenge
afterward.heartbeat.explainer: Enter your master password to prove you're alive. This is the only operation that can reset SOFT_INVESTIGATION back to ALIVE — and the only credential an attacker can't forge (they don't have your password).
afterward.heartbeat.ok: ✓ Challenge passed. If previously in SOFT state, reset to ALIVE.
afterward.vault.title: Vault
afterward.vault.new_hint: New item — path must end with .enc
afterward.vault.content_ph: Write your content here. Saved with AES-256-GCM using your master password.
afterward.vault.explainer: Your encrypted documents. AES-256-GCM, compatible with Obsidian Vault Encryptor.
afterward.vault.hint: Click a file on the left to open it, or click + New encrypted note to start.
afterward.actions.title: Actions
afterward.actions.explainer: One-time deliveries executed after death is confirmed. Each action is a what / to / via / when tuple. The daemon parses this YAML and delivers encrypted vault content via the specified channel to the recipient.
afterward.missions.title: Missions
afterward.missions.explainer: Long-term missions Pi carries on after you're gone. Unlike actions (one-time), missions are perpetual. Each has for whom, trigger, budget, and optional escalation path.
afterward.trustees.title: Trustees
afterward.trustees.explainer: 5 people holding your Shamir shares. Any 3 can reconstruct the vault master key after you're gone. You alone hold the complete password — no one here knows it.
afterward.trustees.hint: Contact editing + heartbeat status supported in v0.5. For now, edit trustees-meta.json directly to update.
afterward.trustees.status: Heartbeat: not tracked · Share: distributed at setup
afterward.drill.title: Drill
afterward.drill.explainer: Run a full lifecycle in isolation: ALIVE → SOFT → DEATH, ~2 seconds (8,640,000× compression, 1 real second = 100 virtual days). End-to-end daemon test, no effect on your real vault.
afterward.drill.passed: ✓ Drill passed. Full end-to-end lifecycle correct.
afterward.audit.title: Audit log
afterward.audit.explainer: Every daemon event, newest first. After you're gone, trustees will see this same log — a tamper-evident record of everything Afterward did for you.
afterward.audit.empty: No events yet. The daemon will write here once started.
afterward.pwdchange.title: Change master password
afterward.pwdchange.explainer: All vault files will be re-encrypted with the new password, and 5 new Shamir shares will be generated. If you've distributed old shares to trustees, you must re-deliver the **new** shares — old shares are void.
afterward.pwdchange.success: ✓ Changed · {n} files re-encrypted · {shares} new shares generated
afterward.pwdchange.err_both_required: Both old and new passwords required.
afterward.pwdchange.err_same: New password must differ from old.
afterward.pwdchange.new_shares_warn: ⚠ **Below are new Shamir shares**. If any trustee previously held old shares, deliver the new ones immediately — **old shares are void**. Distribute all before leaving this page.
afterward.touchid.title: Quick unlock with Touch ID
afterward.touchid.explainer: After enabling, press your fingerprint to unlock Afterward next time — no password typing. Password is encrypted with macOS Keychain; every unlock requires Touch ID verification. Changing master password auto-disables Touch ID.
afterward.touchid.available_off: Touch ID available, but not enabled
afterward.touchid.unavailable: Touch ID or safeStorage not available on this device
afterward.touchid.enabled_now: ✓ Touch ID enabled. You can now unlock with your fingerprint.
afterward.piauth.title: Authorize Pi to read vault
afterward.piauth.explainer: Generate a **time-limited token** (default 30 min) for Pi / Claude Code / other external tools. The token is just a credential — your password never leaves Afterward. Paste the token (not your password) in the Pi conversation; Pi uses the token to call the HTTP API. Tokens auto-expire, and are invalidated when you lock or close Afterward.
afterward.piauth.label: Label
afterward.piauth.none_active: No active tokens. Generate one for Pi.
afterward.piauth.new_token_hint: Paste into Pi conversation. API example: curl -H "X-Afterward-Token: <token>" http://127.0.0.1:17891/afterward/api/list
